title: The Midden's Song
content:
"Bubble, Bubble,
        Swim to see
     Oh, how beautiful
        I be.

     "Fishes, Fishes,
        Finned and fine,
     What's your gold
        Compared with mine?

     "Why, then, has
        Wise Tishnar made
     One so lovely,
        Yet so sad?

     "Lone am I,
        And can but make
     A little song,
        For singing's sake."
meaning:
Walter De La Mare's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Midden's Song"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
